title: Caribbean Examinations Council (Examinations) CAP039A
text:
1 L.R.O. 1979 Caribbean Examinations Council CAP. 39A
(Examinations)
CHAPTER 39A
CARIBBEAN EXAMINATIONS COUNCIL
(EXAMINATIONS)
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
SECTION
1. Short title.
2. Interpretation.
3. Unlawfully obtaining certain papers.
4. Restriction on use of certain papers.
5. Restriction on use of certain information.
6. Removal of certain papers from examination room.
7. Return of papers to Registrar or Police.
8. Disclosures by personnel of Council.
9. Entering examination after acquiring prior knowledge of questions.
10. Impersonation.
11. Forgery of documents.
12. General penalty.
13. Regulations.
THE LAWS OF BARBADOS

by authority of the Government of Barbados

3 L.R.O. 1979 Caribbean Examinations Council CAP. 39A ss. 1-4
(Examinations)
CHAPTER 39A
CARIBBEAN EXAMINATIONS COUNCIL
(EXAMINATIONS)
An Act to provide for the protection of the integrity of examination-s held 1978-26.
by the Caribbean Examinations Council.
[17th August, 19781 Commence-
ment.
1. This Act may be cited as the Caribbean Examinations Short title.
Council (Examinations) Act.
2. For the purposes of this Act- Interpreta-
tion.
“ Council ” means the body recognised by the Government as
the Caribbean Examinations Council;
“ Registrar ” means the Registrar of the Council.
3. A person who, prior to an examination held by the Unlawfully
obtaining
Council, unlawfully procures possession of any paper prepared certain
by the Council containing instructions to candidates or ques- papers.
tions to be answered in that examination is guilty of an offence.
4. (1) A person who, after procuring possession of a paper Restriction
on use of
containing questions to be answered in, or instructions to certain
candidates in connection with, an examination to be held by the papers.
Council-
(a) permits that paper to be seen by any other person;
(b) gives or permits that paper to be given to any other
person ;
(c) discloses the contents of that paper to any other person;
(d) permits that paper or its contents to be used by any
other person; or
(e) makes any other use of such paper or its contents,
is guilty of an offence.
THE LAWS OF BARBADOS
Printed in England by Eyre and Spottiswoode Limited, 2 Scrjeants’ Inn, London EC4,
by authority of the Government of Barbados

ss.5-8 CAP. 39A Caribbean Examinations Council L.R.O. 1979 4
(Examinations)
(2) This section does not apply in respect of a paper given by
an invigilator to a candidate at the time of an examination to
which that: paper relates.
Restriction 5. A person who, after procuring possession of information
on use of
respecting the contents of a paper referred to in section 3 or 4
certain in-
formation. but without obtaining possession of that paper-
(a) gives that information to any other person;
(b) permits that information to be given to any other
person; or
(4 makes any other use of that information,
is guilty of an offence.
Removal of 6. A person who, without the consent of the Council,
certain
removes any examination paper or paper containing instructions
papers
from to candidates from a room in which an examination is held by
examina-
the Council is guilty of an offence.
tion room.
Return of 7. A person who obtains possession of any examination
papers to
paper or paper containing instructions to candidates in con-
Registrar or
Police. nection with an examination to be held by the Council shall
upon obtaining possession thereof return that paper to the
Registrar or the nearest Police Station or make a reasonable
effort to effect its return.
Disclosures 8. A person employed-
by personnel
of Council. (a) in the setting, examining, scoring, moderating, revising,
vetting, printing, collecting, storing, distributing or
keeping the custody of any examination paper;
(b) in the making of a draft examination paper or of a paper
containing instructions to candidates; or
(c) as a supervisor or an examiner, invigilator, attendant or
otherwise,
in connection with an examination held or to be held by the
Council who, prior to or during that examination, knowingly or
recklessly discloses to any other person the contents of such
examination paper, draft examination paper or paper containing
instructions to candidates is guilty of an offence.

5 L.R.O. 1979 Caribbean Examinations Council CAP. 39A ss.9-13
(Examinations)
9. A person who, after acquiring prior knowledge of any Entering ex-
amination
question set in connection with an examination held by the after
Council, presents himself as a candidate at that examination acquiring
prior
is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a
knowledge
fine of five hundred dollars or to imprisonment for six months or of questions
both.
10. A person who, for the purposes of an examination held Impersona-
tion.
by the Council, falsely represents himself to be another person
is guilty of an offence.
11. A person who forges, alters, offers, utters, disposes of or Forgery of
documents.
puts off, knowing the same to be forged or altered, any docu-
ment purporting to show that a person has obtained a result in
an examination held by the Council is guilty of an offence.
12. A person guilty of an offence under this Act for which no General
penalty.
other penalty is provided is liable on summary conviction to a
fine of two thousand dollars or to imprisonment for twelve
months or both.
13. The Council may make regulations generally for carry- Regulations.
ing into effect the provisions of this Act.
THE LAWS OF BARBADOS
